summary: Bird Song brilliance, tight Wheel > Other One, fiery Scarlet Begonias
blurb: The show delivers a soaring Bird Song and an inspired second‑set flow from Scarlet Begonias into Touch of Grey. A thunderous Wheel > Other One jam leads into a moving Morning Dew before the jubilant Sugar Magnolia encore. Jerry’s guitar is on fire despite occasional vocal strain, while the drums shine in a standout Drum > Space segment.
review: On August 30, 1985 at Houston’s Southern Star Amphitheater the Dead turned up the heat both literally and musically. The first set crackles with tight jams – Jack Straw, Row Jimmy and a bright C.C. Rider segue into an exuberant Bird Song that reviewers call “trippy” and “brilliant.” Jerry Garcia’s lead work is fierce, his tone described as “tiger purring and growling,” even if his voice showed signs of strain from the sweltering night. The second set kicks off with Scarlet Begonias flowing straight into Touch of Grey, setting up a tight, almost hypnotic groove that carries through Samson & Delilah into an electrifying Drum > Space sequence – many deem it the best ’85 drumming ever. That momentum builds into a spectacular Wheel > Other One jam, highlighted as “epic” and “inspired,” before winding down with a beautiful Morning Dew and a joyous Sugar Magnolia encore that lifts the crowd. Phil’s bass provides a solid, sometimes trebly foundation, Bob adds his characteristic whizz‑energy on vocals and rhythm, Brent’s keyboards lay down a steady, carnival‑vibe backdrop, and Mickey and Bill deliver powerhouse percussion throughout.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Six out of eight recordings are strongly positive, repeatedly praising Bird Song, Wheel > Other One, the Drum > Space segment and overall tight performance. Only one mixed review notes vocal strain, and a single incoherent negative review provides no substantive criticism. The consistent high praise warrants a large positive sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Crisp soundboard capture with good balance, clear detail across both sets, and the most comprehensive representation of the show’s power despite not being A+ quality.

key_highlights: Bird Song shines with trippy guitar work and tight band interplay.
Wheel > Other One jam is hailed as epic and inspired by multiple reviewers.
Scarlet Begonias into Touch of Grey provides a tight, hypnotic second‑set flow.
Drum > Space segment is frequently called the best ’85 drumming moment.

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar is on fire, delivering trippy leads on Bird Song and a powerful tone described as ‘tiger purring and growling.’ His vocals show strain from heat but his overall presence remains compelling.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh provides a steady bass foundation; noted for a trebly edge that gains low‑end when systems boost around 125 Hz, and a standout intro on the Wheel > Other One jam.
band_performance.Bob: Weir adds solid rhythm guitar and energetic vocals, especially lifting Sugar Magnolia; occasional slight out‑of‑tune twang is mentioned but not detrimental.
band_performance.Brent: Mydland’s keyboards lay down reliable organ/piano textures that support the carnival vibe of the night, complementing the band’s tight jams.
band_performance.Mickey: Hart’s drumming shines in the Drum > Space sequence and the inspired drum solo leading into Wheel > Other One, earning high praise across recordings.
band_performance.Billy: Kreutzmann matches Hart with powerful, driving rhythms; together they create one of the show’s most celebrated moments in the Drum > Space segment.
